join_order.probe: num(filtered: uom=USD, ddate BETWEEN 20230101 AND 20231231, value IS NOT NULL)
join_order.notes: pre_existence_hash mmapped read-only (402MB, capacity=33554432, load_factor=0.286). Add software prefetch: for each row i in batch, issue __builtin_prefetch(&pre_slots[hash3i(adsh[i+P], tag[i+P], ver[i+P]) & pmask], 0, 0) with P=8 before processing row i. This overlaps 402MB random-access LLC misses with computation, hiding ~60ns miss latency per probe.

filters.method: USD is pre-seeded at index 0 in shared/uom.dict; hardcode usd_code=0, skip dict scan at runtime

data_structures.zonemap: flat_array(395 blocks × 10 bytes = 3.9KB, fits in L1; layout: [n_blocks:int32][uom_min:int8, uom_max:int8, ddate_min:int32, ddate_max:int32] per block)
data_structures.antijoin_lookup: pre_built_index(indexes/pre_existence_hash.bin, mmap 402MB, Robin Hood hash, capacity=33554432, load_factor=0.286, linear probe, empty=INT32_MIN); use MADV_RANDOM; add software prefetch with distance P=8 before each probe
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map(key=uint64_t packed (tag_code<<32|ver_code), value=(count:int64, sum:double), capacity=65536 per thread-local map, ~20K expected groups, sentinel=UINT64_MAX)

filter_dimensions: uom: skip block if uom_max < usd_code OR uom_min > usd_code
ddate: skip block if ddate_max < 20230101 OR ddate_min > 20231231

parallelism.scan_parallelism: std::atomic<int32_t> block_cursor; threads steal blocks; each thread owns its thread-local HM
parallelism.antijoin_parallelism: pre_existence_hash is read-only; all threads probe concurrently, no synchronization needed
parallelism.merge_implementation: After scan phase completes (omp barrier), execute log2(nthreads) rounds inside a parallel region. Each round r (0 to log2(nt)-1): stride=1<<r; thread t where t%(2*stride)==0 and t+stride<nt merges tl_maps[t+stride] into tl_maps[t] then frees tl_maps[t+stride]; all threads sync with omp barrier after each round. After log2(nt) rounds, tl_maps[0] holds the complete aggregation. Complexity: O(log N) rounds × O(HM_CAP) per merge, fully parallel, reduces merge wall time from ~24ms to ~1ms.

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks on (uom, ddate); eliminates non-USD and out-of-2023 blocks before any column read
anti_join_lookup; replaces full pre table scan; O(1) average probe per num row with software prefetch

optimization_notes: Root cause analysis identified two independent bottlenecks. (1) build_joins=24ms: the sequential single-threaded merge of 64 thread-local hash maps is a pure parallelism bottleneck (Category D). Total merge work is 64 maps × 65536 slots × 24 bytes = 100MB read plus ~1.28M hash probes, all serialized onto one thread. Replacing with an OpenMP parallel tree-reduction (log2(64)=6 rounds, each round fully parallel with barrier sync between rounds) reduces this to ~1ms wall time since each per-thread merge of a 1.5MB map stays resident in L3. (2) main_scan=40ms: the anti-join probes into the 402MB pre_existence_hash cause ~15.7M LLC cache misses (402MB >> 44MB L3). Adding software prefetch hints (__builtin_prefetch) with lookahead distance P=8 overlaps memory fetch latency with computation on the current row, converting serial miss stalls into overlapped accesses and improving effective throughput. Additionally, USD code is pre-seeded at index 0 so dict scanning is skipped. Combined, these two changes are projected to reduce total time from 100ms to ~75ms, well below the 94ms target.
